questions.q: Shah Jahan is most famous for building which monument?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal at Agra in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.q: Shah Jahan was which number in the line of Mughal emperors?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan was the fifth Mughal emperor of India.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal was built by Shah Jahan in memory of which person?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.q: Shah Jahan was the son and successor of which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan succeeded his father Jahangir as Mughal emperor in 1628.

questions.q: Shah Jahan moved the Mughal capital to a new city he founded in Delhi. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built and moved his capital to Shahjahanabad, the area of Old Delhi.

questions.q: Shah Jahan's reign is especially celebrated as a high point in which field?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's reign is remembered as the golden age of Mughal architecture.

questions.q: What was Shah Jahan's birth name as a prince?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan was born Prince Khurram and took the regnal name Shah Jahan on becoming emperor.

questions.q: Which famous jewelled throne was commissioned by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the celebrated jewel-encrusted Peacock Throne.

questions.q: In which century did Shah Jahan reign over the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan reigned from 1628 to 1658 CE, in the 17th century.

questions.q: Which son of Shah Jahan seized the throne and became the next emperor?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's son Aurangzeb won the war of succession and became the sixth Mughal emperor.

questions.q: In what year did Shah Jahan become Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan ascended the throne in 1628 CE after the death of his father Jahangir.

questions.q: Mumtaz Mahal, in whose memory the Taj was built, died in which circumstances?
questions.explanation: Mumtaz Mahal died in 1631 while giving birth to her fourteenth child, prompting Shah Jahan to build the Taj.

questions.q: Shah Jahan built the famous Red Fort, the Lal Qila, in which city?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Red Fort (Lal Qila) as the palace fort of his new capital Shahjahanabad in Delhi.

questions.q: The great congregational mosque built by Shah Jahan in Delhi is known as which?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Jama Masjid of Delhi, one of the largest mosques in India.

questions.q: Shah Jahan's favourite eldest son, a noted scholar later defeated by Aurangzeb, was which prince?
questions.explanation: Dara Shikoh, Shah Jahan's learned and favoured heir, was defeated and killed by Aurangzeb in the succession war.

questions.q: After being deposed, where did Shah Jahan spend his final years in captivity?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb imprisoned Shah Jahan in the Agra Fort, where he died in 1666.

questions.q: Mumtaz Mahal was the niece of which earlier Mughal queen?
questions.explanation: Mumtaz Mahal was the niece of Nur Jahan, the powerful consort of Jahangir.

questions.q: Shah Jahan's devoted eldest daughter, who cared for him in his captivity, was which princess?
questions.explanation: Princess Jahanara, his eldest daughter, remained with Shah Jahan during his imprisonment at Agra.

questions.q: Shah Jahan's reign saw repeated, costly Mughal campaigns aimed at recapturing which northwestern fortress?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan launched several expensive but ultimately failed campaigns to recover Kandahar from the Persians.

questions.q: In what year did the deposed Shah Jahan die?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan died in 1666 CE while a prisoner of his son Aurangzeb in the Agra Fort.

questions.q: Where is Shah Jahan buried?
options: Beside Mumtaz Mahal in the Taj Mahal
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan was laid to rest beside Mumtaz Mahal inside the Taj Mahal at Agra.

questions.q: The Mughal Empire under Shah Jahan is generally regarded as being at which stage?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's reign is widely seen as the empire's zenith of wealth, splendour, and architectural achievement.

questions.q: The war of succession among Shah Jahan's sons broke out in 1657 after the emperor fell seriously ill with what?
questions.explanation: A serious illness of Shah Jahan in 1657 triggered the succession war among his four sons, won by Aurangzeb.

questions.q: Shah Jahan built a magnificent white marble mosque inside the Agra Fort known by which name?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Moti Masjid, or Pearl Mosque, of gleaming white marble within the Agra Fort.

questions.q: Which two younger brothers did Aurangzeb defeat or eliminate, besides Dara Shikoh, in the succession struggle?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb overcame Shah Shuja and Murad Baksh, alongside Dara Shikoh, to seize the throne.

questions.q: Before becoming emperor, Prince Khurram earned the title 'Shah Jahan' from his father after a successful campaign in which region?
questions.explanation: Jahangir bestowed the title Shah Jahan on Prince Khurram after his Deccan campaigns.

questions.q: Shah Jahan annexed which Deccan sultanate outright in 1636 during his southern expansion?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's forces extinguished the Nizam Shahi sultanate of Ahmadnagar, annexing it to the empire in 1636.

questions.q: Shah Jahan's ill-fated Central Asian campaign of the 1640s targeted which region, the ancestral Mughal homeland?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's costly Balkh and Badakhshan campaign aimed to recover the Timurid homelands but ended in withdrawal.

questions.q: The European traveller who left a vivid eyewitness account of Shah Jahan's court and the Peacock Throne was which jeweller?
questions.explanation: The French jeweller Jean-Baptiste Tavernier described Shah Jahan's court and the jewel-laden Peacock Throne in detail.

questions.q: The architect most often credited in tradition with the design of Shah Jahan's Taj Mahal is named as which?
questions.explanation: Ustad Ahmad Lahauri is traditionally regarded as the chief architect of Shah Jahan's Taj Mahal.
